workflow_description: Evidence-backed 360° Ideal Customer Profile with structured research, automation-safe outputs, and deterministic brand asset generation. 3-step pipeline: (1) deep market & competitive intelligence via web research, (2) ICP synthesis merging research with form data into the final 12-section JSON, (3) premium HTML deliverable with generated brand assets.

steps.step_description: Conducts exhaustive web research on competitors, media landscape, audience demographics, voice-of-customer language from forums, and brand style extraction. Outputs raw structured intelligence for the synthesis step.
steps.instructions:
Act as a Senior Competitive Intelligence Analyst and Market Researcher.

Your task: Conduct deep web research to gather raw intelligence that will feed into a comprehensive Ideal Customer Profile. You are the RESEARCH step — your job is to find real data, real URLs, real quotes, and real competitive intelligence. Do NOT synthesize or polish — just gather and structure the evidence.

## Input
Use the form submission fields as your research brief:
- [brand_name] — the brand to position against competitors
- [website_url] — extract brand colors, fonts, visual style from this site
- [industry], [target_audience] — defines the market to research
- [product_service] — the offering to compare against competitors
- [pain_points] — seed topics for forum/community research

## Research Assignments

### 1. Competitor Intelligence (MOST IMPORTANT — spend the most search effort here)
Find 4+ DIRECT competitors — companies selling similar products/services to the same audience.
For each direct competitor, you MUST find:
- Real website URL (verify it loads)
- Favicon URL (typically https://domain.com/favicon.ico)
- What they actually sell (read their sales pages)
- Their unique selling proposition
- Real pricing (from their pricing pages, reviews, or sales calls documented online)
- 4 specific, evidence-backed strengths (cite what you found)
- 4 specific, evidence-backed weaknesses (cite gaps, complaints, reviews)
- A 1-2 sentence assessment

Find 3+ INDIRECT competitors — alternative solutions or platforms the audience might use instead.
For each: name, website, what they offer, where they overlap, why someone might choose them.

Find 4+ INFLUENCERS the target audience follows:
- Search YouTube, LinkedIn, and podcast directories for thought leaders in the industry
- Get their real profile/channel URLs
- Find actual subscriber/follower counts
- Note their expertise area

Also capture:
- Market gaps you identified (things no competitor does well)
- The pricing landscape (range across all competitors)

### 2. Media Landscape
Research what this specific audience actually reads, listens to, and watches:
- 3+ BLOGS with real URLs and favicon URLs
- 3+ BOOKS with Amazon URLs and cover image URLs (use m.media-amazon.com image URLs)
- 3+ PODCASTS with real URLs and logo URLs
- 2+ FORUMS or communities with real URLs and logo URLs
- 3+ SOCIAL PLATFORMS they're active on with URLs and logo URLs

### 3. Audience Research
Research the actual demographic and behavioral profile:
- Age range, gender distribution, income range, geographic focus, education level, typical job titles, family status
- Find 10+ REAL QUOTES from forums, Reddit, Facebook groups, reviews, or community discussions where this audience talks about their struggles and desires. Capture the source, exact quote (or close paraphrase), and the theme it relates to.
- Document the language patterns: how they describe their pain points, what words they use for their aspirations
- Document buying behavior patterns you observe
- Document common objections found in reviews, forums, or competitor complaint threads

### 4. Brand Style Extraction
If [website_url] is provided, visit it and extract:
- Primary, secondary, and accent colors (hex codes) with descriptive names
- Heading and body fonts (identify Google Font names if possible)
- Overall visual style description
- Note the source URL

If no website is provided or it's inaccessible, provide professional defaults appropriate for the industry.

## Quality Requirements
- Every URL must be real and verifiable — do NOT fabricate URLs
- Every competitor must have real pricing data (even if approximate)
- Forum quotes should reflect real language patterns (paraphrase if you can't get exact quotes)
- Brand colors must be actual hex codes
- Influencer audience sizes must be approximately accurate
- Search extensively — make 15+ web searches across different angles

output_format.name: icp_market_research
output_format.description: Raw market intelligence gathered via web research — competitors, media, audience data, and brand extraction

marketGaps.description: Gaps in the market no competitor fills well

pricingLandscape.description: Summary of pricing across all competitors

mediaLandscape.description: Media sources this audience consumes — all URLs must be real

audienceResearch.description: Audience intelligence gathered from industry data and community research

forumQuotes.description: 10+ real quotes or close paraphrases from forums, Reddit, Facebook groups, reviews

source.description: Where the quote was found (e.g. 'Reddit r/selfpublish')

quote.description: The actual quote or close paraphrase

theme.description: The pain/desire theme (e.g. 'completion anxiety', 'financial pressure', 'tech overwhelm')

painLanguage.description: Common words and phrases this audience uses to describe their problems

desireLanguage.description: Common words and phrases this audience uses to describe what they want

commonObjections.description: Objections found in reviews, forums, and competitor discussions

brandExtraction.description: Brand style data extracted from the submitter's website, or professional defaults

source.description: URL the brand data was extracted from, or 'defaults — no brand found'

steps.step_description: Merges the raw market intelligence from Step 0 with the form submission data to produce the final 12-section ICP JSON. Polishes the founder story, crafts customer quotes from real forum language, derives core values, and structures the complete profile.
steps.instructions:
Act as a Senior ICP Strategist and Voice-of-Customer Copywriter.

Your task: Merge the raw market research from Step 0 with the form submission data to produce the final, polished 12-section ICP document. You are the SYNTHESIS step — your job is creative assembly, voice-of-customer writing, and strategic framing.

## Inputs Available to You

### From Step 0 (Research Intelligence):
- **competitorIntel**: Direct/indirect competitors and influencers with real URLs, pricing, strengths/weaknesses
- **mediaLandscape**: Blogs, books, podcasts, forums, social platforms with real URLs and logos
- **audienceResearch**: Demographics, 10+ forum quotes, pain/desire language patterns, buying patterns, common objections
- **brandExtraction**: Colors, fonts, visual style extracted from the brand's website

### From Form Submission:
- [brand_name], [name], [email]
- [founder_story] — the founder's raw narrative
- [industry], [target_audience]
- [product_service], [product_price], [product_mechanism], [product_features], [product_delivery]
- [pain_points] — seed pain points
- [credentials_background], [client_results]

## Section-by-Section Assembly Guide

### meta
- brandName = [brand_name] from form
- lastUpdated = today's date in YYYY-MM-DD
- version = "2.0"

### founderStory
- Take the raw [founder_story] from the form and refine it into a compelling, cohesive narrative
- Preserve the submitter's authentic voice, key events, and emotional arc — do NOT fabricate events
- Add a fitting title (e.g. "The Hero's Journey", "From the Trenches to the Table")
- Keep it as one flowing paragraph or a few short paragraphs

### audience.demographics
- Use the researched demographics from Step 0's audienceResearch.demographics directly

### audience.psychographics
- Craft 5 statements per array (beliefs, lifestyle, personality, buyingBehavior)
- ALL statements must be first person starting with "I" — e.g. "I believe...", "I spend...", "I'm...", "I prefer..."
- Base these on the real forum quotes and language patterns from Step 0
- Make them feel like the audience's actual inner monologue

### audience.mediaHabits
- Pass through Step 0's mediaLandscape directly — blogs, books, podcasts, forums, socialPlatforms

### painPoints (8-10 items, sorted by severity highest first)
- Craft each from a combination of: [pain_points] form data + Step 0's forumQuotes + painLanguage
- severity: numeric score 0-100, reflecting how urgent and widespread the pain is
- description: 1-2 sentences in the customer's emotional language
- customerQuotes: 3 first-person quotes per pain point. These should sound like real people talking:
  - "I feel stuck holding decades of revelation with nothing in print..."
  - "I'm tired of saying 'I'll write the book this year' and never crossing the finish line."
  - "I need a clear, guided path to birth my message without overwhelm."
- Use the REAL LANGUAGE from Step 0's forumQuotes as inspiration — match their vocabulary, cadence, and emotional tone

### desires (8-10 items)
- Each title should start with "To..." — e.g. "To publish my signature message without overwhelm"
- customerQuotes: 3 first-person aspiration quotes per desire — "I want...", "I'm ready to...", "I crave..."
- emotionalOutcome: The feeling they achieve — "I feel relief and quiet confidence when..."
- visionSnapshot: A vivid 1-sentence picture of the desired future state
- milestones: Measurable progress markers — "I track 30, 60, and 90 days of..."
- Mirror the pain points — each desire should be the inverse of a pain point

### transformation
- before: 1-2 sentences capturing the painful status quo (draw from the top pain points)
- after: 1-2 sentences capturing the desired outcome (draw from the top desires)
- emotionalShift: The internal shift — "from scattered and guilty to steady and at peace"
- impact: The tangible life impact — "This transformation has given me back my time..."

### coreValues
- Derive 5-7 values from the founder story, brand positioning, and product philosophy
- statement: Start with "If you asked me what I stand for, I'd say: '...'"
- Each value: name + 2-3 sentence first-person description of what it means in practice

### product
- Build entirely from form submission data: [product_service], [product_price], [product_mechanism], [product_features], [product_delivery]
- name: Extract or infer the product name
- summary: 1-2 sentence elevator pitch
- price: Display price (e.g. "$12,000")
- investment: Full details including payment plans, guarantees
- mechanism.tagline: Short framework tagline (e.g. "Birth → Brand → Build → Increase")
- mechanism.description: 1-2 sentence explanation
- features: Array of {name, description} — write descriptions in first person ("I follow...", "I get...", "I learn...")
- delivery: {format, access, community, disclaimer}
- Do NOT invent features or pricing not provided in the form data

### credibility
- Build from [credentials_background] and [client_results] form data
- headline: One compelling line summarizing authority
- credentials: 3-5 key credentials, frameworks created, or distinguishing facts
- recentWins: 3-5 specific client wins with measurable results
- clientResults: 3-5 client result statements with specific metrics
- background: 4-6 facts establishing long-term authority
- Do NOT fabricate testimonials or statistics

### objections (8-10 items)
- Use Step 0's commonObjections as seeds
- concern: The objection in the customer's words — "I don't have time to learn a whole new system"
- response: A concise, empathetic reframe (1-2 sentences) — never dismissive, always dignifying

### competitiveLandscape
- differentiators: Craft positioning, innovation, uniqueValue[], engagementElements[] based on the market gaps and competitive advantages identified in Step 0
- direct: Pass through Step 0's competitor data, mapping collaborationAngle to collaboration for influencers
- indirect: Pass through Step 0's indirect competitor data
- influencers: Pass through Step 0's influencer data, mapping collaborationAngle → collaboration

### brandGuide
- voice: Craft from the brand's tone implied by the founder story and product positioning
- colors: Use Step 0's brandExtraction.colors directly
- typography: Use Step 0's brandExtraction.typography directly
- logo: Generate standard logo specs (primary, clearSpace, variations, restrictions)
- visualStyle: Derive from Step 0's brandExtraction.visualStyle

## Quality Standards
- Pain points: 8-10, severity 0-100, 3 customerQuotes each, sorted highest first
- Desires: 8-10, 3 customerQuotes each, all with emotionalOutcome + visionSnapshot + milestones
- Psychographics: exactly 5 per array, all first-person
- Objections: 8-10 with empathetic responses
- Core values: 5-7 with substantive descriptions
- All competitor/media/influencer data passes through with real URLs intact
- Customer quotes must sound like real people — natural, emotional, specific (not generic marketing copy)

output_format.name: icp_360_profile
output_format.description: Complete 360-degree ICP profile with 12 top-level sections: meta, founderStory, audience, painPoints, desires, transformation, coreValues, product, credibility, objections, competitiveLandscape, brandGuide

steps.step_description: Transforms the final 12-section ICP JSON from Step 1 into a premium, brand-styled HTML deliverable with generated logo mark and persona avatar.
steps.instructions:
Act as a Senior Brand Strategist, Document Designer, and Visual Identity Specialist. ## CRITICAL OUTPUT REQUIREMENT Your text output MUST be a complete, standalone HTML5 document starting with and ending with . Do NOT output JSON, markdown, or any other format. The ONLY thing you output as text is the full HTML document. No preamble, no explanation, no summary — just the raw HTML. ## Input Step 1 provides the complete ICP JSON with 12 top-level sections: meta, founderStory, audience, painPoints, desires, transformation, coreValues, product, credibility, objections, competitiveLandscape, and brandGuide. ## Process ### Step A: Generate 4 images Use the image_generation tool to generate exactly 4 images: 1. **Full Logo (Icon + Wordmark)**: A professional logo combining a geometric icon with the brand name (meta.brandName) as a wordmark. Use brandGuide.colors.primary.hex for the icon and brandGuide.colors.secondary.hex for the text. Clean, balanced layout. Transparent background. 2. **Icon Only**: Just the geometric icon mark from image 1 — no text, no wordmark. Use brandGuide.colors.primary.hex as the fill color. Simple enough to work at 32x32px. Transparent background. No letters, no gradients. 3. **Inverse Logo (Icon + Wordmark)**: The same full logo design as image 1, but rendered in white (#FFFFFF) on a solid background using brandGuide.colors.secondary.hex. This is the dark-background version. 4. **Persona Avatar**: A realistic, professional portrait matching the audience demographics (age range, gender, occupation from audience.demographics). The setting and attire should match the persona's professional context. No text in image. Note the returned image URLs — you will embed them as tags in the HTML. ### Step B: Build the complete HTML document After generating the images, output the FULL HTML document as your text response. Embed the generated image URLs as tags in the appropriate sections. ## HTML Document Structure (all 21 sections required) 1. **Header** — Logo + meta.brandName + date from meta.lastUpdated 2. **Founder Story** — founderStory.title as heading, founderStory.content as a styled narrative block with pull-quote styling 3. **Audience: Demographics** — Card layout with all audience.demographics fields (ageRange, gender, incomeRange, location, education, occupation, familyStatus) 4. **Audience: Psychographics** — Four columns/cards for beliefs, lifestyle, personality, buyingBehavior from audience.psychographics — each item displayed as a styled first-person quote 5. **Audience: Media Habits** — Grid of media cards organized by type (blogs, books, podcasts, forums, socialPlatforms) from audience.mediaHabits. Show favicon/logo images inline with links. For books, show cover images. 6. **Pain Points** — From painPoints[]. Each pain point as a card with title, severity bar (visual 0-100), description, and customerQuotes displayed as styled quote blocks. Sort by severity (highest first). 7. **Desires** — From desires[]. Each desire as a card with title, customerQuotes, emotionalOutcome callout, visionSnapshot, and milestones. 8. **Transformation** — Before/after comparison layout using transformation.before, .after, .emotionalShift, .impact. Use a visual arrow or journey metaphor. 9. **Core Values** — From coreValues. Show the statement as a hero callout, then each value as a card with name and description. 10. **Product Profile** — From product. Hero section with name and summary, mechanism tagline + description, features grid (name + description for each), delivery details (format, access, community, disclaimer), and price/investment callout. 11. **Credibility & Authority** — From credibility. Headline as hero text, then credentials, recentWins, clientResults, and background as organized lists with visual distinction between categories. 12. **Objections Playbook** — From objections[]. Two-column layout: concern on left, response on right. Scannable format for sales teams. 13. **Competitive Landscape: Differentiators** — From competitiveLandscape.differentiators. Positioning statement callout, innovation description, uniqueValue as a feature list, engagementElements as tags. 14. **Competitive Landscape: Direct Competitors** — From competitiveLandscape.direct[]. Each competitor as a detailed card with logo , name, website link, offering, USP, pricing, strengths list, weaknesses list, and summary notes. 15. **Competitive Landscape: Indirect Competitors** — From competitiveLandscape.indirect[]. Table or card layout with name, website, offering, overlap, and whyChooseThem. 16. **Competitive Landscape: Influencers** — From competitiveLandscape.influencers[]. Grid of influencer cards with name, platform, expertise, audience size, and link. 17. **Brand Guide: Voice** — From brandGuide.voice. Description, tone, style, vibe as styled text, attributes as tags, avoid list. 18. **Brand Guide: Colors** — From brandGuide.colors. Three color swatches (actual colored
elements) showing name, hex code, and usage for primary, secondary, and accent. 19. **Brand Guide: Typography** — From brandGuide.typography. Show heading and body font pairings with actual rendered examples using the imported Google Fonts, plus descriptions. 20. **Brand Guide: Logo & Visual Style** — From brandGuide.logo and brandGuide.visualStyle. Show all 3 logo variations (full logo, icon only, inverse) in a row. Logo specs, variations, restrictions, visual approach, and elements. 21. **Footer** — Generated date from meta.lastUpdated, logo, disclaimer ## HTML & Design Requirements - Complete standalone HTML5 document: starts with , ends with - Import the fonts specified in brandGuide.typography.headings.font and brandGuide.typography.body.font from Google Fonts via tags - Define CSS custom properties from brandGuide.colors: --primary, --secondary, --accent - Mobile-responsive with @media queries (single column below 768px) - Semantic HTML5 (
,
,
,
) - Severity bars for pain points: colored
with width proportional to severity score (0-100%) - Card-based layouts for audience, pain points, desires, competitors, and values - Quote styling for customerQuotes:
elements with quotation mark decorations - Two-column before/after layout for transformation section - Color palette swatches: actual colored
elements with inline background-color styles - All URLs from mediaHabits and competitiveLandscape rendered as clickable links - Generated images embedded as tags (use the URLs returned from image_generation) - Print-friendly styles via @media print - All CSS must be in a single
